findings.evidence: `rg -n "change |show " code/TauCeti/AlgebraicTopology/FundamentalGroup/BasepointChange.lean` finds line 101: `change (Subgroup.normalizerQuotientEquivMap H ...).symm`; nearby generic API has `Subgroup.normalizerQuotientEquivMap_symm_mk` at `code/TauCeti/Algebra/Group/NormalizerQuotientConjugation.lean:139`.
findings.fix: Replace the `change` with an explicit congruence/rewrite lemma for this inverse basepoint-change representative calculation, or add a short comment explaining why this definitional conversion is unavoidable before the `change`.
findings.issue: `basepointChangeNormalizerQuotientEquiv_symm_mk` relies on a large bare `change` to expose the mapped subgroup form before applying `Subgroup.normalizerQuotientEquivMap_symm_mk`, making the proof depend on undocumented definitional equality across `basepointChangeSubgroup`/`H.map` and `MulEquiv.subgroupCongr`.

summary: One proof uses an undocumented `change` to bridge a definitional-equality gap in the inverse quotient representative lemma. The rest of the added proofs are short rewrites over existing transport lemmas.
